TextENGNew: I saw a pure white ceiling. The fluorescent lights were faintly flickering.
I could faintly smell the peculiar odor of chemicals.
Was this the nurse's office...? No, the room was too big and too quiet.
In fact... was this even the school at all? And what time was it...?
I tried turning my head a little to the side. ...The windows were covered by thick curtains. Traces of orange light seeped through the gaps, but I wasn't able to see outside.
I reluctantly sat up to get out of bed, but was thrust down as though there were a weight on the left side of the blanket, returning my head back to the pillow.
Right then, the person sleeping at my bedside woke up and looked at me. ...It was Chisato-chan.
"Chisato-chan...?"
Chisato-chan's eyes overflowed with huge tears the moment she saw my face. Then she lunged forward and hugged me for dear life.
"Kyah?!@k@vS04/36/310100741. Ch-Chisato-chan, why...?"
"I'm glad... so glad...!@k@vS04/37/310300474. You really... really had me worried sick...@k@vS04/37/310300475. Uuuh!!"
The warmth of her arms and her choked voice... gradually removed the fog from my mind, and brought back my memories.
Right... I went with Chisato-chan to the care center...
And then......
"......Sorry, Chisato-chan.@k@vS04/36/310100744. I'm sorry... I didn't mean to scare you."
"It's ok... It's ok... *sob* ...!"
Chisato-chan nodded over and over, but refused to loosen her grip around me. However, it didn't feel like the usual hug she did as a joke. No, this one was very... very gentle.
"...I'm the one who should be sorry...!@k@vS04/37/310300478. I should have tried harder to stop you...@k@vS04/37/310300479. I'm sorry I couldn't help you... I'm sorry..."
"No... it's not your fault.@k@vS04/36/310100746. So you don't have to cry anymore... ok?"
Chisato-chan was always cheerful and full of laughter.
Honestly, part of me thought she was just making fun of me, but... now I knew that I was mistaken.
Even though she usually joked around, I was deeply grateful for how much she worried and cared about me.
"...Hey, Chisato-chan.@k@vS04/36/310100748. Where am I?"
"...In a hospital room at Saeki Hospital.@k@vS04/37/310300481. It was so sudden, so this was the only room available..."
I gently pulled myself away from Chisato-chan's chest and took another look around.
...It was a spacious and clean private room with only one bed. There was nothing very "only room available" about it. Chisato-chan must have demanded to her father that I be brought here.
Come to think of it... what time is it...?
"Right now, it's 6 o'clock.@k@vS04/37/310300483. ...Oh, you can take it easy.@k@vS04/37/310300484. I told your mom that we're having a study session at my house, and that you'd be staying the night."
Chisato-chan also mentioned that she was able to keep my part-time job a secret.
"...I'm sorry. About anything and everything."
"It's fine Natsumi. Don't worry about a thing.@k@vS04/37/310300486. This is about all I'm good for right now, anyway."
"No... that's not true."
Because I can't even do a single thing that Chisato-san did.
Right... In the end, I couldn't do anything. In fact, my actions might have done far more harm than good.
Back then... if I hadn't stood up for old man Hatakeyama, he could have been transferred to the psychiatric ward and received proper treatment.
Then Yamase-san wouldn't have been killed, and everything could have been resolved peacefully...
"It really wasn't a dream after all, was it..."
Understanding the meaning of the words I muttered to myself, Chisato-chan sat silently with a grave expression. She didn't say anything, but that reaction was answer enough.
"...About Yamase-san."
Instead, she said that quietly and shifted her eyes toward the window curtains.
"She was an excellent nurse. She even used to be the head nurse at Saeki Hospital.@k@vS04/37/310300490. She was the one who originally proposed establishing the care and welfare center."
...A few years ago, Yamase-san's family consisted of her husband, her son, and her father.
Her father was old and bedridden. To make ends meet, Yamase-san apparently did her job both at the hospital and at home, every day.
But... her workload got even heavier after she was promoted to head nurse.
Nursing is already the kind of profession where you have to work irregular shifts to provide patients with 24 hour care, and being a manager on top of that leaves you with little to no free time.
...For that reason, she couldn't do much at home, so her husband and son decided to help take care of her father.
However... an unfortunate accident occurred.
One day when her father was sick with a fever, her son just administered vitamin supplements like it was any other cold, but... the cause was actually organ failure.
Unfortunately, the family didn't realize it for a while, so by the time his condition suddenly changed and they brought him to the hospital, it was already too late... Her father passed away...
"...Yamase-san never blamed her son.@k@vS04/37/310300492. Rather, she regretted not properly teaching him about the symptoms her father had displayed at the time.@k@vS04/37/310300493. And then..."

"When it comes to caring for the elderly, civilians without medical knowledge tend to take inadequate measures.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1071. Plus, caregiving is just as hard a job as nursing, and it places a greater burden on your family as well."
"That's why I think we need a facility that teaches the elderly the skills to live a normal life, as opposed to the hospital, whose objective is centered around treatment."
"...With that, she proposed the establishment of the care and welfare center to my father, and they built a facility to care and rehabilitate the elderly."
"And she worked hard every day so those who worked in and out of medicine would acknowledge and understand the contributions and importance of caregivers! So why...!?"
Chisato-chan grit her teeth at that point. She clenched her fists so tight that they were turning white, and her nails began to dig in.
"She didn't deserve to die so horribly!@k@vS04/37/310300497. She worked her hardest for so many people!@k@vS04/37/310300498. She deserved to be acknowledged by others and be happy!"
"Then that crazy bastard just had to barge in and tear it all to shreds!!@k@vS04/37/310300500. Damn it! Damn it!!"
"......Chisato-chan..."

"And then he clawed out his throat and died anyway?!@k@vS04/37/310300502. Screw that!@k@vS04/37/310300503. If he wanted to die so bad, he should have just killed himself and left it at that!!@k@vS04/37/310300504. But instead, he went and killed hardworking Yamase-san on top of that!@k@vS04/37/310300505. And what's more, he got you involved too!!"
"Hinamizawan bastards!!@k@vS04/37/310300507. I don't know what this curse nonsense is all about, but it doesn't give them the right to screw up other people's lives!!"
I silently wrapped my hands around Chisato-chan's trembling hands.
She was always so kind and cheerful.
She was my dear friend who was genuinely concerned for me and shed tears when I woke up.
Yet that very same girl was now full of rage and hatred, sparing not a single word in voicing her enmity for Hinamizawa.
After seeing that... I don't know what I'm thinking anymore.
Empathy as a close friend?
Sympathy as a person?
Or... pangs of conscience as someone from the very same place as the one I should hate?
I don't know... I don't know what to feel anymore...
And then...
Someone knocked on the door. Chisato-chan took a breath, changed her expression, and responded in a stiff tone born from tension.
"......Who is it?"
"...It's Minai, from Kakiuchi Station.@k@vS04/28/310500222. Is this a bad time?"
Chisato-chan gripped my hands tight and looked into my eyes. ...She whispered "Should I turn them away?" to me, but I slowly shook my head, and then nodded.
"......Please come in."
"Pardon the intrusion..."
And just a moment later, I heard the rattling of the doorknob as the door opened. Standing on the other side with a firm expression was Detective Minai, wearing the same red suit from before.
"...Are you feeling better, Natsumi-san?"
"Are you kidding me...?"

Chisato-chan sounded like she was struggling to conceal her anger. However, Detective Minai seemingly paid her no heed and quietly walked into the room, passing right by her until she was right at my bedside, and turned to me.
"Hey, hold on..."
"I'm sorry for asking this of you right after you woke up.@k@vS04/28/310500226. Could you spare a moment?@k@vS04/28/310500227. ...Natsumi Kimiyoshi-san."
"Y-you've got some nerve!@k@vS04/37/310300514. Do you have any idea how Natsumi was feeling just a minute ago?!"
"I'm aware of that.@k@vS04/28/310500229. ...But I do have a job to do, so I'd appreciate your cooperation."
"......Chisato-chan."
I grabbed Chisato-chan's arm just as she was about to lunge at Detective Minai with fury. I looked at her for a few seconds, then turned toward Detective Minai and began to speak.
"If possible... can we do this in private?"
"Yes.@k@vS04/28/310500231. That would be a big help on my end too.@k@vS04/28/310500232. ...I'll make my best effort not to stress out Natsumi-san. Is that acceptable with you?"
"Chisato-chan... it'll be fine."
I said that while smiling and holding Chisato-chan's hand.
...Chisato-chan hesitated for a while, but eventually agreed and stood up, perhaps out of consideration for my wishes.
...But just before she opened the door and left the room, she stared daggers at Detective Minai.
With Chisato-chan gone, the room suddenly got quiet. I sat myself upright, grimacing from a dull pain in my head.
"Are you feeling ok?"
"I'm fine... probably."
"There are... several things I'd like to ask, but please do not hesitate to stop me if things get too painful for you.@k@vS04/28/310500235. ...Oh, may I sit down?"
"Go ahead."
After thanking me, Detective Minai sat down on a folding chair. She began to speak, her face and tone softening compared to a minute ago.
"I'm so sorry.@k@vS04/28/310500237. ...For getting rough with you, I mean.@k@vS04/28/310500238. Is your neck feeling ok?"
"Uh, umm..."
I gently ran my hand along my neck. It felt a bit off, but nothing serious.

"If you'd kept shouting like that, you could have lost your voice.@k@vS04/28/310500240. Furthermore, I wanted to keep the crowd from panicking. So I got rough with you.@k@vS04/28/310500241. I went too far, I admit it."
I smiled awkwardly and and nodded halfheartedly. I'd thought she was a cold person, but the meek look on her face made my impression of her as a scary person fade slightly.

"That girl... Saeki-san... had some rather harsh words for me.@k@vS04/28/310500243. She told me she didn't know what she'd do to me if something were to happen to you, and simply claiming responsibility would be nothing more than an excuse.@k@vS04/28/310500244. She was absolutely right, so I made no attempts to defend myself."
"That happened...?@k@vS04/36/310100763. But I... don't really remember any of that."
"I remember everything up to seeing a bunch of police cars parked in front of the center and calling out to you,@k@vS04/36/310100765. ...but everything after that... is a little fuzzy."
"...Perhaps the mental shock has left your memories in temporary disarray.@k@vS04/28/310500246. But you don't have to force yourself to remember. Whatever you can do now should be sufficient."
I made up my mind and asked. I knew it would sound suspicious, but... I still couldn't believe it.
"Is Yamase-san,@k@vS04/36/310100769. ...really dead...?"
Detective Minai cocked her head in surprise for a moment, looked back and forth as if carefully considering her words, took a deep breath... and quietly nodded in affirmation.
"When the police arrived, she was in a state of cardiac arrest.@k@vS04/28/310500249. Forensics determined that she suffered severe trauma to the back of her head... Death was almost instantaneous."
"......Is that so...?"

"I'd like to say it's good she didn't suffer in her final moments... but that would be arrogant coming from the living.@k@vS04/28/310500251. It was our... no, my own mistake here that caused this tragedy."
"I know an apology won't solve anything, but please just allow me to say it.@k@vS04/28/310500253. ...I'm sorry."
I silently watched as Detective Minai bowed her head.
...Strangely enough, I felt no anger nor sadness, even though I was crying and shouting at her just a few hours ago.

"...Honestly, I was being naive.@k@vS04/28/310500255. I heard Hatakeyama was kind by nature, so I simply assumed he wasn't capable of such brutality...@k@vS04/28/310500256. If I'd kept a closer eye on him, all of this could have been...!"
"...What happened to old man Hatakeyama?"
"......@k@vS04/28/310500258. Will it do you much good to learn?"

"I doubt it.@k@vS04/36/310100774. ...But you're going to question me after this, right?@k@vS04/36/310100775. Then please tell me what you know too.@k@vS04/36/310100776. I... am already connected to this."
I thought maybe I was being audacious, but... I refused to concede on this point.
If I didn't learn anything about the circumstances, this might just end with me mourning Yamase-san's murder and hating old man Hatakeyama for it... and nothing more.
I didn't want that. Both of them were close to me, so I wanted to learn everything I could.
"...Do you promise?"
After staring at me while thinking for a while, Detective Minai finally said that. I nodded, understanding she was asking me not to tell anyone else.
"It's not a pleasant story... Are you absolutely sure?"
Detective Minai heard my reply, then took a notepad from her breast pocket and opened it. Then, she began talking in a voice devoid of emotion.

"Kazuma Hatakeyama broke into the caregivers' waiting room around 11 in the morning.@k@vS04/28/310500263. The victim, Kazuko Yamase, was taking a phone call in there. Hatakeyama lifted a fire extinguisher that he found in the room, then struck her on the back of her head and killed her."
"...Forensics reported that the cause of death was due to a skull fracture and damage to the cervical vertebrae induced by the blow to the head..."
It happened right after the other caregivers had left to clean up the patients' breakfast.
They said Yamase-san returned to the waiting room to respond to a call from the hospital's nurse center.
It was a report that Hatakeyama-san had disappeared from the hospital room where his fever was being treated.
According to eyewitness reports from other caregivers, Yamase-san was surprised to hear that, having not seen him at breakfast, and proceeded to call Hatakeyama-san's family.
The phone in the waiting room was on the opposite side of the entrance, meaning her back was turned to it. So she sat in the chair, unable to hear the door opening due to the sound of the phone.
She was struck hard from behind by the fire extinguisher... and that was the end of her.
"Are you all right?@k@vS04/28/310500266. ...Is this too much for you?"
"No, I'm fine.@k@vS04/36/310100781. ...Please continue."
Noticing that I was clenching the bed sheets slightly, Detective Minai briefly stopped talking out of concern. But I shook my head and urged her on.
...The pang of irritation I'd felt wasn't because I found the story painful. Rather, it was the opposite... it was because I didn't feel anything about it.
Why? ...A person I know was killed... Shouldn't I be sad?

"Then the suspect took an all-purpose knife from the cupboard in the washroom... After staying there for a while, he fled to his own room in the center.@k@vS04/28/310500268. ...And at 12:37, he injured himself in that room."

"The police rushed over as soon as they received the call, secured him, and brought in an emergency physician... but the bleeding was severe, and his death was confirmed at 1:51 PM.@k@vS04/28/310500270. ...That is all."
Detective Minai probably intentionally omitted the violence Hatakeyama-san inflicted on Yamase-san's body.
However, I had no need to confirm that...... As for why, it was because I could no longer recall the state of her corpse.

In the meantime, everyone else either watched him from a distance or locked themselves in their own rooms, fearing they'd be next. As a result, no one tried to stop him. Apparently, some of the caregivers fled their workplace in fear.
...That was probably the obvious reaction. If I were there, I would have run away crying and hid myself somewhere safe, trembling with fear.
All I would have had to do was deny it, and no one could prove otherwise.
......Or maybe not?
Would I really have run away if I were there...?
I mean, old man Hatakeyama shouldn't have been able to kill me...... Moreover, even if he turned his blade on me... he wouldn't be the old man I knew.
In that case... If he was just an impostor of old man Hatakeyama... I'm sure I could have......!
"...Kimiyoshi-san?"
"Oh, I'm sorry.@k@vS04/36/310100783. ...Umm, you mentioned something about the suspect injuring himself just now.@k@vS04/36/310100784. ...Was that also with the knife?"
"No.@k@vS04/28/310500273. He threw the knife out his window as soon as he returned to his room.@k@vS04/28/310500274. ...The murder weapons were his own fingernails."
"......Fingernails?"
"Right.@k@vS04/28/310500276. He held his fingers to his throat... and scratched repeatedly with all his strength.@k@vS04/28/310500277. Hard enough to tear through his skin and the flesh beneath."
It didn't make any sense.
Even if he came to regret committing murder and chose to atone for it by taking his own life... why wouldn't he do it using the sharper weapon?

"That's right.@k@vS04/28/310500279. However, the suspect's mental derangement seems to be corroborated by eyewitness testimony, @k@vS04/28/310500280. and when I actually met him the other day, his abnormal behavior, such as his deep-seated obsession with worshipping Oyashiro-sama, was plain to see."
"So I determined it would be difficult to explain his actions considering the illogical basis they stemmed from."
Fingernails are hard. They're softer than bones, of course, but they're the second hardest part of the human body.

...However, they are not very sharp, especially compared to metal cutlery, so even though the throat is a vulnerable area, it would have taken time to make an incision and he would have been feeling severe pain the whole time.
Considering all that, I couldn't think of a single reason he'd choose to use his nails.
"...Do you think you've heard enough?"
After saying that, Detective Minai closed her notebook and held it to her mouth. ...I still didn't feel like I had all the pieces, but I nodded and answered "yes" for the time being.
"Well then, here's a question from me.@k@vS04/28/310500284. ...It concerns that "Oyashiro-sama" I mentioned just now."
"Oyashiro-sama...?"

"Right, the guardian god that has been worshipped in Hinamizawa village since ancient times.@k@vS04/28/310500286. ...The elderly have apparently been blaming Oyashiro-sama's curse for the disaster that occurred the other day. What does that mean?"
"......Please promise me something first."
I raised my head and stared at Detective Minai.
"What is it...?"
"I'll tell you everything I know right here, right now.@k@vS04/36/310100791. So even if you ask me for more, you will not get anything else."
"Also, even if you ask others from Hinamizawa, I don't think you'll get anything more than what I'm about to tell you."
"...So what's the promise?"
"After this, please stop asking me and the people in my life about Hinamizawa.@k@vS04/36/310100794. I'd like this to be the last time."
Right... Honestly, I'd had enough. I was fed up.
I've been caught in more trouble than I should have and felt more pain than I should have, all thanks to my connection with Hinamizawa.
That's why... I wanted to end it all here. Because I had nothing to do with Hinamizawa or the curse...
"......Understood. You have my word.@k@vS04/28/310500291. This is the first and last time I will talk with you about Hinamizawa.@k@vS04/28/310500292. Is that all right?"
"Sorry about that.@k@vS04/36/310100796. ...Then let's talk.@k@vS04/36/310100797. You wanted to know about Oyashiro-sama, didn't you?"
Then I retraced what I could remember and began to speak. ...Even though my head had been all hazy just a few moments ago, now it oddly felt refreshingly clear.
"My Grandma says that the people who abandoned Hinamizawa village are responsible for the Hinamizawa gas disaster, that it's Oyashiro-sama's curse."

"Oyashiro-sama does not allow the villagers to leave the village. Anyone who breaks that rule will be cursed and misfortune will befall them.@k@vS04/36/310100800. ...So apparently, this incident is the result of that."
"Abandoning the village, and leaving for the outside world,@k@vS04/28/310500294. ...does that even include simply moving, or work-related relocation?"

"Not only that. I heard that even traveling was unacceptable until a little while ago.@k@vS04/36/310100802. That's why they say those who leave the village without permission will start hearing the footsteps of Oyashiro-sama following them."
"I haven't heard it myself, but when one of those people walk down the street,@k@vS04/36/310100804. ...they supposedly hear an extra footstep."
"What a bizarre god...@k@vS04/28/310500296. Does this curse they're so afraid of really exist?"
"I,@k@vS04/36/310100806. I don't really believe it, honestly.@k@vS04/36/310100807. And most of the young children probably feel the same way."
"...But ever since the Watanagashi curse began, the elderly people have redoubled their faith and grown tremendously fearful of Oyashiro-sama."
"Since the Watanagashi curse began...?"
Detective Minai seemed a little dubious, but she was writing my story down in her notepad.
"Well then, next question.@k@vS04/28/310500299. About the victims of this curse on the night of the Watanagashi each year..."
"......You're well-informed.@k@vS04/36/310100810. But I was in Okinomiya, so I don't know too much..."
"I do not mind.@k@vS04/28/310500301. Tell me, please."
After saying that, she leaned forward and looked closely at me. I didn't have much interest in it, but I told her as much as I knew for the time being.

"Four years ago, during the campaign against the construction of the Hinamizawa dam project.@k@vS04/36/310100812. A relative of mine, the mayor of the village, was in command of it.@k@vS04/36/310100813. ...The foreman of the construction site was killed on the night of Watanagashi.@k@vS04/36/310100814. The culprits were said to be other construction workers at the site."

"That incident was even reported in newspapers over here.@k@vS04/28/310500303. I heard that the criminals dismembered the corpse, and each concealed a piece.@k@vS04/28/310500304. ...However, all except one of them were arrested. The leader of the incident escaped, and is still at large even now."
...That reminded me.
At the time, some weekly magazine wrote an amusingly exaggerated article about the village and the tragedy.
It's unclear whether that was the trigger, but the dam construction plan was completely reset, so the incident was considered a blessing to the village.

"Since the incident involved the murder of a construction official from the dam project the people of Hinamizawa were fighting against, the villagers somewhat jokingly called it the work of a "curse".@k@vS04/36/310100816. So I don't think anyone back then held much fear or reverence for Oyashiro-sama."
Yes... At that time, the existence of Oyashiro-sama and the "curse" were only symbols for the villagers to unite behind.

Although Watanagashi was indeed honored as a source of spiritual support, it was very small compared to the festivals near Okinomiya. I don't think it'd be wrong to say only a few took it all that seriously.

"The situation changed the following year.@k@vS04/36/310100818. The Houjou couple, the leaders of the pro-dam faction who were rumored to be receiving payments from the government, died on the day of Watanagashi.@k@vS04/36/310100819. They accidentally fell from a cliff."
On the day of Watanagashi... Houjou and his wife went for a family drive. They allegedly left their daughter in the car and went out to enjoy the scenery.
Then the fence they were leaning on collapsed... and both of them fell into a mountain stream. The corpse of the husband was located, but his wife was never found.

"It's a very callous thing to say,@k@vS04/36/310100821. ...but most of the villagers were thinking "serves them right."@k@vS04/36/310100822. I wasn't all that fond of the husband in the Houjou family either.@k@vS04/36/310100823. And then, villagers who'd been on the fence took this opportunity to merge with the anti-dam faction, ending the conflict that had divided the village."
However... They never reconciled with the surviving members of the Houjou family. Their children, who were about my age, continued to receive the cold shoulder from the villagers.
To tell the truth, I felt sorry for them. The parents may have betrayed the village, but the rest of their family was another matter entirely.
Needless to say, their children had nothing to do with it, so Mion-chan and Rika-chan angrily complained how unfair it was.
...Still, the mayor would get in a bad mood whenever I brought it up with him, so I stopped talking too much about it.
I wasn't acquainted with them, so I eventually resigned to stand aside, and I'd almost forgotten it until now...

"And then came the following year.@k@vS04/36/310100825. ...This time, the heads the Furude family, who'd maintained a moderate stance even as a part of the anti-dam faction, perished.@k@vS04/36/310100826. The husband died of an illness, and his wife committed suicide by drowning herself."
"I think it was around this time everyone in the village began to fear the curse of Oyashiro-sama."

"They believed that taking a moderate attitude,@k@vS04/36/310100829. ...meant not seriously respecting Oyashiro-sama, the symbol of the anti-dam faction.@k@vS04/36/310100830. So if those two had been punished for that, that would make all the unfaithful just as susceptible to Oyashiro-sama's curse."

"So most of the villagers became devout believers to avoid suffering the same fate,@k@vS04/36/310100832. and they began celebrating the Watanagashi festival on a grand scale.@k@vS04/36/310100833. My Grandma also..."
"...Grandma?"
"Ah, nevermind."
For a moment, Detective Minai's tone dropped a little lower. I didn't want to discuss Grandma's recent delusions, so I brushed the whole thing aside and continued explaining.

"And last year, the housewife of the Houjou family, who did not participate in Watanagashi, was killed.@k@vS04/36/310100836. ...At the same time, the son went missing.@k@vS04/36/310100837. And this year..."
"...A traveling photographer was killed,@k@vS04/28/310500308. and a nurse in the village clinic who was on close terms with him was burned out in the mountains... right?"
It was my turn to be surprised. ...Only those from Hinamizawa should have known about it. My own family only just found out about it after getting in touch with our relatives.

"Actually, I heard about this year's incident from a detective in Okinomiya Station.@k@vS04/28/310500310. And I was one of the people present when the nurse was found in the mountains.@k@vS04/28/310500311. So, that's why."
"Then... you knew about the Watanagashi murder cases?"
"I heard a little from that detective.@k@vS04/28/310500313. But thanks to you, I learned some valuable information.@k@vS04/28/310500314. Thank you for your help."
Detective Minai answered in anticipation of my question, closed her notepad, and put it in her pocket.
Then she leaned back in her chair to relieve her tension, turned away, and sighed. She then noticed the clock sitting on the small table next to my bed, picked it up, and checked the time.
"...I've taken a lot of your time, huh?@k@vS04/28/310500316. Are you feeling all right?"
I actually felt slightly ill, but I shook my head. Seeing that, Detective Minai faintly smiled and got up out of her seat.
"Thank you for your cooperation.@k@vS04/28/310500318. Also, it's not my duty to say this, but... take care of yourself."
"Oh... Umm."
I called out to Detective Minai just as she politely bowed to me and motioned to leave the room.
I nearly got back when she shot me a quizzical look, but I took a breath and told her to repeat what I asked earlier.
"...Please, let this be the last time.@k@vS04/36/310100844. Please."
"I understand.@k@vS04/28/310500320. I know I may not look like it, but I hate lying.@k@vS04/28/310500321. That includes breaking promises, of course."
"Also,@k@vS04/36/310100846. ...could you not tell anyone I am from one of the Three Families of Hinamizawa?"
For a moment, Detective Minai looked at me peculiarly. But she quickly picked up on my intentions and nodded firmly.

"Understood.@k@vS04/28/310500324. I'm aware that unusual prejudices against Hinamizawa have been spreading recently.@k@vS04/28/310500325. I promise you.@k@vS04/28/310500326. I will not let my tongue slip. Not even to Saeki-san."
"...Thank you very much."
"Well then, I will speak with Saeki-san on my way out.@k@vS04/28/310500328. ...Get some rest today."
Detective Minai left the room. ...I was was left all alone in the large hospital room.
Why?
Ever since a little while ago... my heart and mind had felt light... like they'd cleared up.
The same went for my sadness, anger, and warm feelings... They were all gone.
Yamase-san had been murdered. Hatakeyama-san was dead. Chisato-chan had erupted with anger. Detective Minai was desperately trying to solve the mystery.
Yet for some reason... it didn't feel like... any of it mattered.
Then... I removed all the heavy stones from my head and began to think clearly... One reliable answer came to mind.
"......That's right."
The reason I had been depressed and suffering up until now. ...It was because I came from Hinamizawa.
Lives had been lost in the blink of an eye.
Efforts had gone to waste just like that.
Telling others you were from Hinamizawa only brought misfortune... and the loss of two lives.
In that case... the solution was very simple.
I just had to cease being a Hinamizawan.
Then I wouldn't have to suffer from this detestable inferiority complex.
And if I wasn't a member of the Three Families, then those who feared the curse would no longer mean anything to me.
How... how easy...... What a simple solution...!
Guilt?
Pricks of conscience?
Ahahahahahahahaha, what good would any of that do?
If I hadn't stood up for old man Hatakeyama... that crazy old man who went insane from fear of the curse, Yamase-san wouldn't have had to die, would she?
What good was whitewashing things?
What would a show of false kindness accomplish?
"Nothing...!@k@vS04/36/310100851. Right, it accomplishes nothing!@k@vS04/36/310100852. Ahahahahaha... Aaahahahahahahahahahahah!!"
That detective promised me I would no longer have anything to do with this.
And I told her everything. Everything I knew about Hinamizawa.
No... that's not right. I didn't tell her about Hinamizawa. I threw away Hinamizawa.
Things that should be kept secret, things that should have invoked divine punishment, everything... Everythiiing!!
So I'm no longer a Hinamizawan...!
There is no curse! Those who fear it are of no concern to me!
And so, with each slander I throw Hinamizawa's way...
My heart fills with joy, clears up, grows lighter, and shines brighter.
"Ahahahahahahahahahahah!!@k@vS04/36/310100854a. Aaaahahahahahahahahah!!!"
That's it. I will keep things the way they are now.
My dear friends. The person I love. Everything and everyone that makes me happy.
For that reason, I'll harden my heart and strengthen it more than ever...
Hinamizawa... was no longer my problem.
And in this moment where the sound of the higurashi gradually faded away... where I sat amidst the vermillion light fading into darkness...
I felt happier than I ever had before.
